Module 1: introduction

Learn to talk to almost any AI model (like ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini, Llama, DeepSeek...) using code, and do it in a smart, clean way that doesn't break when you switch models.

Why should we learn this?

A few real situations I've seen (and you probably will too):

  • Company says: "Use Claude now, it's cheaper/better/safer"
  • OpenAI raises prices → you want to switch to Groq + Llama fast
  • You want function calling / tools but some models do it differently
  • You built something nice with GPT-4o → now you want to test it with Gemini 1.5 or DeepSeek for free/cheap
  • You want memory + tools + external APIs (like searching Google, sending emails, checking database) and it should work with any model

If you write code locked to OpenAI only → you suffer every time the price or rules change.

So we learn to write code once → then switch models with basically one line.

What we'll actually build step by step

Think of it like levels in a game:

  1. Just send message → get reply (any model)
  2. Make the AI give perfect JSON every time (no random text mess)
  3. Teach it to use tools (calculator, search, your own functions)
  4. Add memory so it remembers previous messages
  5. Add long-term memory (save important facts forever)
  6. Handle rate limits, retries, cheap fallbacks automatically
  7. Put everything together → build something useful like research helper, email drafter, or personal assistant

Quick look how the flow usually looks (real apps)

Here's a simple picture version many people use:

(LLM in middle + memory + tools + planning loop, very common pattern)

And another one — long-term memory part:

Big benefit people want in 2025-2026

  1. One codebase → many models (no rewrite)
  2. Before = pain (different code everywhere)
  3. After = clean (one nice gate/unified way)

And fun part, some people even imagine teams of different AIs working together

Module 2: Mastering LLMs & APIs – Connect to Any Model, Add Tools & Memory

Goal

  1. Write code once → talk to GPT-4o / Claude 4 / Gemini 2 / Llama 4 / DeepSeek / Grok etc.
  2. Send structured requests → get reliable JSON/tool calls
  3. Add tools (search, calculator, your shop database)
  4. Add memory (remembers customers)
  5. Handle rate limits, retries, cheap fallbacks

Build production-ready agents without rewriting code every time prices change or a new model drops.

Why this really matters nowadays:

Your shop gets 20–30 WhatsApp messages/day:

"Screen tod gaya bhaiya, kitna time lagega?"

You reply manually → slow + tired.

The AI replies politely in Hindi/English, checks price/stock, books slot, remembers "Raman prefers Samsung parts", switches to cheaper DeepSeek when busy.

1. Setup (Do Once – 5 Minutes)

Install core libs:

pip install litellm python-dotenv pydantic langchain langchain-community chromadb requests tenacity

Create .env file in your project folder (never commit to git!):

OPENAI_API_KEY=sk-...

ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=sk-ant-...

GEMINI_API_KEY=AIza...

GROQ_API_KEY=gsk_...

TOGETHER_API_KEY=...

# Add more as needed (DeepSeek, Fireworks etc.)

Load them safely:

from dotenv import load_dotenv

load_dotenv()

Get free keys today:

  • OpenAI → platform.openai.com
  • Anthropic → console.anthropic.com
  • Google Gemini → ai.google.dev
  • Groq (fast open-source) → console.groq.com
  • Together.ai / Fireworks.ai for more open models

2. Core Idea: Unified LLM Caller (Write Once, Switch Forever)

LiteLLM makes 100+ models look like OpenAI format.

# llm.py – your central file

from litellm import completion

import os

def ask_llm(

prompt_or_messages,

model="openai/gpt-4o-mini", # ← change ONLY here!

temperature=0.7,

max_tokens=500,

tools=None, # for function calling later

response_format=None # for structured JSON

):

if isinstance(prompt_or_messages, str):

messages = [{"role": "user", "content": prompt_or_messages}]

else:

messages = prompt_or_messages

try:

response = completion(

model=model,

messages=messages,

temperature=temperature,

max_tokens=max_tokens,

tools=tools,

response_format=response_format,

api_key=os.getenv(f"{model.split('/')[0].upper()}_API_KEY") # auto picks key

)

return response.choices[0].message

except Exception as e:

print(f"Error with {model}: {e}")

return None

Test it:

msg = ask_llm("One fun fact about Bhopal?")

print(msg.content)

Now change model= to:

  • "anthropic/claude-3-5-sonnet-20241022" (or newer Claude 4 variants)
  • "gemini/gemini-1.5-flash" (or gemini-2.0)
  • "groq/llama-3.1-70b-versatile" (super fast)
  • "deepseek/deepseek-chat" (cheap reasoning)

Same code — different brain. That's the win.

Here’s a visual of what this unification looks like in practice:

3. Structured Outputs – Guaranteed JSON (No More Parsing Mess)

Use Pydantic + response_format.

from pydantic import BaseModel, Field

class RepairReply(BaseModel):

greeting: str = Field(description="Polite hello in Hindi+English")

model_ask: str = Field(description="Ask for phone model if missing")

price_quote: str | None = None

slot_suggest: str | None = None

response = ask_llm(

"Customer: Screen broken. Reply helpfully.",

model="openai/gpt-4o-mini",

response_format={"type": "json_schema", "json_schema": RepairReply.model_json_schema()}

)

print(response.content) # valid JSON every time

Claude/Gemini support similar via tools or json mode.

4. Tool / Function Calling (AI Uses Calculator, Search, Your APIs)

Define tools like OpenAI format (LiteLLM normalizes for all).

tools = [

{

"type": "function",

"function": {

"name": "get_current_price",

"description": "Get repair price for phone model",

"parameters": {

"type": "object",

"properties": {"model": {"type": "string"}},

"required": ["model"]

}

}

},

{

"type": "function",

"function": {

"name": "book_slot",

"description": "Book repair slot for customer",

"parameters": {

"type": "object",

"properties": {

"customer_name": {"type": "string"},

"time": {"type": "string"}

},

"required": ["customer_name", "time"]

}

}

}

]

# In ask_llm pass tools=tools

Loop example (simple ReAct-style agent):

def run_agent(query, model="groq/llama-3.1-70b-versatile"):

messages = [{"role": "user", "content": query}]

while True:

resp = ask_llm(messages, model=model, tools=tools)

messages.append(resp)

if not hasattr(resp, 'tool_calls') or not resp.tool_calls:

return resp.content # final answer

for tool_call in resp.tool_calls:

func_name = tool_call.function.name

args = json.loads(tool_call.function.arguments)

if func_name == "get_current_price":

# fake db call

result = f"₹{1500 if 'samsung' in args['model'].lower() else 2000}"

elif func_name == "book_slot":

result = f"Slot booked for {args['time']}"

else:

result = "Tool not found"

messages.append({

"role": "tool",

"tool_call_id": tool_call.id,

"name": func_name,

"content": result

})

print(run_agent("Fix my iPhone screen tomorrow morning?"))

This flow (very common agent pattern):

5. Memory Integration

Short-term (conversation history):

Use list of messages — just keep appending.

Long-term (persistent facts, customer prefs) – use Chroma vector store + RAG.

from langchain_community.vectorstores import Chroma

from langchain_openai import OpenAIEmbeddings # or use free HuggingFace embeddings

embeddings = OpenAIEmbeddings()

vectorstore = Chroma("shop_memory", embeddings)

# Save fact

vectorstore.add_texts(["Raman prefers fast delivery and Samsung parts."])

# Retrieve before asking LLM

docs = vectorstore.similarity_search(query, k=3)

context = "\n".join([d.page_content for d in docs])

full_prompt = f"Context from memory:\n{context}\n\nUser: {user_query}"

Short V/S Long Memory:

6. Rate Limits, Retries, Fallbacks & Cost Optimization

Use tenacity for retries:

from tenacity import retry, stop_after_attempt, wait_exponential

@retry(stop=stop_after_attempt(5), wait=wait_exponential(multiplier=1, min=2, max=10))

def safe_ask_llm(...):

...

Fallback routing:

models_to_try = [

"openai/gpt-4o-mini",

"groq/llama-3.1-70b-versatile",

"deepseek/deepseek-chat"

]

for m in models_to_try:

try:

resp = ask_llm(..., model=m)

if resp: return resp

except:

continue

Current rough pricing/context (March 2026 snapshot):

  • GPT-4o family: ~$0.15–$2.50 input / $0.60–$10 output, 128k–1M context
  • Claude 4 variants: ~$3–$15 / M tokens, 200k context
  • Gemini 2.x: ~$0.30–$10, up to 1M+ context
  • Groq Llama/DeepSeek: 10–50× cheaper, 128k–2M context on some

7. Putting It All Together: Mini Shop Agent Example

Combine everything:

# shop_agent.py

def shop_agent(user_query):

# Retrieve long-term memory

docs = vectorstore.similarity_search(user_query, k=2)

memory_ctx = "\n".join(d.page_content for d in docs) or "No prior info."

system = f"You are helpful repair shop assistant in Bhopal. Use Hindi+English. Memory: {memory_ctx}"

messages = [{"role": "system", "content": system}, {"role": "user", "content": user_query}]

final = run_agent(messages, model="groq/llama-3.1-70b-versatile") # cheap & fast fallback

return final

Run: print(shop_agent("My Samsung screen cracked, book slot?"))

Bonus: Local / Open-Source (No API Cost)

Use Ollama for fully local:

# Install Ollama → ollama.com

ollama run llama3.1

Then in LiteLLM: model="ollama/llama3.1"

Or Groq for free/fast inference.
